texts.facebook:
Your A/B test says 'winner!' — but is it lying to you? 🤔

Today's ML interview question: Design an A/B test for a new recommendation model. Which mistakes most often break such tests?

Strong answer: lock your North Star metric (like revenue, not just CTR) before launch, randomize by user, size the test with power analysis, and never peek early. Most tests die from optimizing a proxy metric or forgetting guardrails like latency.

Practice this exact question in a full mock interview — Junior track is free: https://ml-simulator-app-production.up.railway.app/en.html?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=organic&utm_content=2026-07-18-interview-9d8984
texts.linkedin:
Interview question of the day (Middle ML Engineer track):

Design an A/B test for a new recommendation model. Which mistakes most often break such tests?

A strong answer covers four pillars: a North Star decision metric (like revenue per session) fixed before launch, with CTR as a proxy and guardrails like latency and session length; randomization by user with a check for spillovers; sample size and duration set by power analysis, not by 'it turned significant'; and an A/A test to confirm the split works.

Where these tests usually break: peeking too early, non-random assignment (by city or platform), chasing a proxy metric while the North Star quietly drops, and forgetting guardrails while 'winning' on paper.

Want to practice this out loud, in a real mock interview? The Junior track is free — start here: https://ml-simulator-app-production.up.railway.app/en.html?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=organic&utm_content=2026-07-18-interview-9d8984

#MachineLearning #MLEngineer #DataScience #ABTesting #TechInterview
texts.tiktok: A/B test 'wins' but revenue tanks? 😅 That's the #1 mistake in ML interviews. #ml #machinelearning #techcareer Link in bio

card.headline: Design an A/B Test for a Recsys Model
lines: Lock North Star metric before launch
Size test via power analysis, no peeking
Run an A/A test to verify the split
